title: 5.3.3 Bellman-Ford Algorithm
subtitle: Shortest paths with negative edges and negative-cycle detection

content.introduction: Bellman-Ford finds single-source shortest paths even with negative-weight edges and can detect reachable negative cycles.

sections.title: 5.3.3.1 Key Ideas and Model
sections.content: Define repeated edge relaxation over V-1 rounds and the dynamic-programming intuition behind path-length bounds.
examples: Explain why V-1 rounds are sufficient without negative cycles

sections.title: 5.3.3.2 Representation and Workflow
sections.content: Describe initialization, full-edge scans, predecessor updates, and final extra scan for negative-cycle detection.
examples: Run Bellman-Ford on a graph with one negative edge
Mark nodes affected by a detected negative cycle

sections.title: 5.3.3.3 Complexity and Practical Guidance
sections.content: Complexity is O(VE), higher than Dijkstra but more general due to negative-edge support.
examples: Compare runtime on sparse vs dense graphs
Use Bellman-Ford when correctness with negatives is required
Evaluate trade-off between generality and speed

keyTakeaways: Support shortest paths with negative edges
Choose Bellman-Ford for correctness-critical weighted graphs
